page: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-44 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance featuring 44 vCPUs and 176 GB of RAM. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 2.7 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 22 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled and dedicated CPU allocation. The processor features a cache hierarchy consisting of a 48 KB L1 data cache, a 2 MB L2 cache, and a 105 MB L3 cache. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPU accelerators.

Benchmark results indicate strong performance in specific workloads. The instance achieves a PassMark CPU score of 47631.73 and a PassMark CPU encryption test score of 38725.32, both falling into the strong category. Static web serving throughput is also strong at 41.93 GB/sec. In LLM inference tests using a small 135M model, the server processes prompts at 2194.89 tokens/sec and generates text at 285.08 tokens/sec, both rated as strong. However, performance degrades on larger models; while prompt processing for a medium 7B model remains strong at 187.15 tokens/sec, text generation for a large 70B model drops to a poor rating of 2.39 tokens/sec.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c3-standard-44 offers a balanced resource density of 4.0 GB of memory per core, making it suitable for standard enterprise applications without the premium cost of memory-optimized tiers. The inclusion of advanced instruction sets, such as Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512), provides hardware-accelerated execution for cryptography and vector operations without requiring expensive GPU hardware. A key tradeoff is the complete lack of local storage, meaning all storage operations must rely on network-attached volumes, which may impact I/O-heavy workloads.

This instance is well-suited for general-purpose enterprise workloads, static web serving, software compilation, and moderate database operations. The presence of AMX and AVX-512 instructions also makes it viable for small to medium machine learning inference tasks, though it is not recommended for large-scale LLM text generation due to the lack of GPU acceleration and the observed performance drop on 70B models.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-44 is a general-purpose instance featuring 44 vCPUs and 176 GB of RAM, powered by a 2.7 GHz Intel Xeon 8481C processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture with 22 physical cores and dedicated CPU allocation, it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. The instance demonstrates strong performance in static web serving (41.93 GB/sec), CPU encryption, and small-to-medium LLM inference, achieving 2194.89 tokens/sec for 135M model prompt processing. However, text generation for large 70B models is a notable bottleneck, dropping to 2.39 tokens/sec. Qualitatively, the server provides balanced resource density and cost-effective compute power via Intel AMX and AVX-512 extensions, making it highly suitable for enterprise web serving, software compilation, and moderate database operations.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-44 is a general-purpose server with 44 vCPUs, 176 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering strong performance for web serving and medium-sized model inference.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-44 features 44 vCPUs and 176 GB RAM, delivering strong performance for web serving and cryptography workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and advanced matrix extensions for accelerated enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 2.7 GHz Intel Xeon 8481C processor with 44 vCPUs and 176 GB RAM.
Features Intel AMX and AVX-512 extensions for accelerated cryptography and vector math.
Delivers strong static web serving throughput of 41.93 GB/sec.
Performs strongly on small-to-medium LLM inference but struggles with large 70B models.
Lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, requiring network-attached storage.
Best suited for enterprise web serving, software compilation, and moderate database tasks.
